channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Schools face challenges teaching children how to identify and handle misinformation without getting political, especially in recent days as false claims swirl surrounding the recent hurricanes and the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Google monopoly: Prosecutors hint at company breakup, but analysts say it’s unlikely
item.description: The case was filed in 2020, heralding a new U.S. government legal effort to rein in tech giants, with anti-monopoly cases against Amazon, Apple, and Facebook and Instagram parent firm Meta.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Elon Musk is hampering hurricane relief efforts—and using X to do it
item.description: When Federal Emergency Management Agency first responders raced to western North Carolina in the days after Hurricane Helene, they expected much of the devastation they found in its wake: totaled cars, crumbling roads, homes filled with mud or ruined by floodwaters or washed away entirely.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: The IRS is rolling out its free tax-filing tool to 30 million Americans—and surprisingly, it’s great
item.description: Some 30 million Americans across the country will have a new way to file their taxes next spring: Direct File, a tool developed by the Internal Revenue Service that allows users to prepare their taxes and file online completely free of charge and without the need for any third-party provider.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Covert, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla’s Cybercab, Robovan revealed with Elon Musk’s reputation at stake
item.description: The product launch in California didn't address how Tesla will make the leap from selling advanced driver-assistance features to fully autonomous vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Tesla Unveils Robotaxi and Self-Driving Van; Elon Musk Enters Politics - Electric Future Ahead
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk reveals robotaxi and self-driving van, emphasizing safety and cost-effectiveness, amidst his increasing involvement in US politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's 'Cybercab' as stock sinks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tesla unveiled its long-awaited robotaxi at a Hollywood studio Thursday night, though fans of the electric vehicle maker will have to wait until at least 2026 before they are available. CEO Elon Musk pulled up to a stage at the Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER and ALEX VEIGA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AMD unveils new generation of Epyc, Instinct chips
item.description: AMD announced its latest AI and high-performance computing processors at its Advancing AI event in San Francisco, including the fifth generation of its Epyc server processors and AMD Instinct MI325X accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Several Bay Area counties are among the most at risk in the U.S. for natural disasters. One of them is trying to improve its preparedness.
item.description: Alameda, Santa Clara and Contra Costa counties are facing the 4th, 5th and 8th highest risk within California, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Katie Lauer, The Mercury News

item.title: Senators press Commerce Department to boost transparency about CHIPS Act contracts
item.description: A group of senators are urging the Commerce Department to be more transparent regarding the terms of agreements made under the CHIPS and Science Act, arguing the agency must ensure the U.S. semiconductor industry is “safe and sustainable.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ben Horowitz’s dual support for Trump and Harris says a lot about the Valley’s AI politics
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Marriott settles with the FTC for $52 million over data breaches
item.description: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and make changes to bolster its data security to resolve state and federal claims related to major data breaches that affected more than 300 million of its customers worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Deepfakes and Elections: German Marshall Fund Launches Tracking Tool Amid Global Election Year
item.description: The German Marshall Fund has launched a tool to track deepfakes created by AI targeting elections, as more than half the globe holds or will hold elections in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Wimbledon tennis tournament replaces human line judges with electronic system
item.description: Wimbledon is replacing line judges with electronic line-calling, the latest step into the modern age by the oldest Grand Slam tennis tournament.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft brings Copilot AI tools to OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot is now available in OneDrive, part of a wider revamp of the company’s cloud storage platform. Microsoft highlighted several ways to use the generative AI (genAI) assistant in OneDrive in a blog post Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people
item.description: "We could view cybersecurity as an entry level problem, but it is probably more of an intermediate and advanced role problem," Keith Clement said. The post A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: The Red Sea Cable Cuts: A Wake-Up Call for Resilient Global Networks - RETN Report Reveals 70% Disruption Level
item.description: RETN's network diagnostics and customer feedback expose the extent of disruption caused by February 2024 cable cuts in the Red Sea, highlighting the need for a more resilient global network infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Inflection AI shifts to Intel Gaudi 3, challenging Nvidia’s AI chip lead
item.description: Inflection AI is shifting its latest enterprise platform to Intel’s Gaudi 3 accelerators, stepping away from Nvidia GPUs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
